decisions.what: Used gh api --include for ETag support
decisions.why: gh api supports --include to get response headers, needed for ETag-conditional polling

decisions.what: Separate jq extraction per item type (runs vs issues/pulls)
decisions.why: Actions runs have different JSON structure (workflow_runs wrapper, actor vs user, no labels)

decisions.what: Atomic state updates via tmp file + mv
decisions.why: Prevents corruption if script is killed mid-write

discoveries: gh api --include returns headers + body separated by blank line, usable for ETag extraction
summary: Built fleet-watch-github, a 135-line bash script that polls GitHub issues, PRs, and failed CI runs via gh api with ETag-conditional requests. Writes new events to bridge watch-events/ directory, appends to watch-briefing.md, sends Telegram notifications, and tracks seen IDs in watch-state.json to avoid reprocessing.
